Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

The principle of a Sanyassi not meeting the King

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-08-29 · 3 answers
What can you explain about this principle? Is Raganugha or vaidhi-bhakti institutionalized through it when it so happens inside the varnashrama-dharma as suggested? And how.

Thanks.
user [38] · 2010-08-29
This is a prescription on the material dharma level, as far as I can ascertain.
user [154] · 2010-08-30
Sannyasi or brahmacari also not supposed to look in the face of a women (any women). A king is always engaged in sense-gratification so better not to see such people if you are a sannyasi, it will spoil your austerity.
user [560] · 2010-09-05
The principle automatically suggests that as the King so are the subjects and that is the only one allowed to kill say for means of politics thus, there is a difference on that aspect of the material level for the Dharma means because it does not mean that because the King is responsible for the entire social body to work that you can assure that the King is allways engaged in sense gratification say a woman is so then, if the King should kill an animal was it a ferocious animal by the forest and not a rabbit to prove its killing prowes of power so, what is the job of a sanyassi on varnashrama then it so questions that and there are the two kind of level thoughts, say for example daivic-varnashrama and the asuric-varnashrama dharma, on this two the sanyassi dharma and the king'b4s dharma go side by side, isn'b4t it? OR not. In one way the sense gratification level is important but is not the moto so how would they be considered certainly? Austerity also is as very important. If the King is so much so realized then He will ensure every varna and ashrama is working as it should on a social body which works with it, and so..
